source: Per-row citations below. ASB declared distributions fetched 2026-06-15 from the ASNB FY2025 income-distribution announcement (https://www.asnb.com.my/V2/pdf/asb/press%20release_asb%20income%20distribution_2025.pdf and asnb.com.my income-distribution pages), corroborated by The Edge Malaysia (theedgemalaysia.com/node/786562) and RinggitPlus FY2025 distribution write-up. 12-month fixed-deposit board rates fetched 2026-06-15 from RinggitPlus FD comparison (https://ringgitplus.com/en/fixed-deposit/12-month/). EPF declared dividends from owned corpus/data/datasets/epf-dividend-history.json (verified 2026-05-21) — not refetched. Malaysian equity realized-return anchor and active-fund dispersion from owned corpus/data/datasets/us-vs-my-returns.json (FBM KLCI factsheet, verified 2026-06-11) and owned corpus/proof/fund-universe-stats-snapshot.md (Apr 2026 weighted-alpha screen) — not refetched.
notes: Inputs for a RISK-RETURN POSITIONING chart, NOT a returns ranking (SC App.1 §I1a 'sufficiently similar features' / §I1b undue prominence; EngineerDad EPF/ASB comparison rule). The instruments do different jobs at different risk tiers and must not be lined up as a single winner table. Two parts: (1) ASB declared annual distributions by year (sen per unit on the fixed RM1.00 ASNB price ≈ % return; ASB is principal-stable — fixed-price, never a negative distribution year on record — but the return is NON-GUARANTEED and declared annually by PNB); (2) four positioning rows giving each tier a return figure and a documented relative capital-risk index for the x-axis. The capital-risk index (1 = principal-stable / statutory floor, 5 = full market risk) is a DOCUMENTED QUALITATIVE ordering, not a sourced market statistic — it encodes capital-loss exposure, not volatility math. Honest core, visible in the figures: over recent windows EPF (~5.5-6.3%) and ASB (~5.0-5.75%) have returned MORE than broad Malaysian equity's realized capital return (FBM KLCI 1.2% pa 5y, 6.7% pa 3y), so equity does NOT tower over the capital-stable tier — the equity band OVERLAPS it and carries real downside (universe median drawdown -3.93% from each fund's high, deepest on file -49.32%). The legitimate equity case is long-horizon growth potential and active alpha (Apr 2026 equity-fund median weighted alpha +2.04 vs benchmark), accepting that the return is uncertain and can be negative in any year — never a claim that UT is safer than or beats EPF/ASB. EPF carries the EPF Act 1991 §27 guaranteed-minimum dividend (2.5% nominal). All figures flagged volatility 'variable' (declared/board rates change); re-verify before reuse after 2027-06-15.

data.principal_basis: Fixed-price RM1.00 unit (NAV does not fluctuate); principal-stable, distribution non-guaranteed, declared annually by PNB
data.source: ASNB FY2025 income-distribution press release (asnb.com.my/V2/pdf/asb/press release_asb income distribution_2025.pdf, fetched 2026-06-15); The Edge Malaysia 'PNB declares RM10.4b in dividends or 5.75 sen a unit for flagship ASB' (theedgemalaysia.com/node/786562); RinggitPlus 'ASB FY2025 Distribution' (fetched 2026-06-15)

data.breakdown: 3.35 sen dividend + 1.25 sen bonus + 0.50 sen additional bonus

data.capital_basis: Capital-guaranteed nominal; PIDM-insured up to RM250,000 per depositor per bank; contractual fixed rate for the tenure
data.job: Parking / capital preservation / near-term liquidity buffer
data.source: RinggitPlus 12-month FD comparison (https://ringgitplus.com/en/fixed-deposit/12-month/, fetched 2026-06-15): conventional 12-month board rates ~2.00-2.55% p.a., promotional campaigns to ~3.0-3.2%. PIDM coverage RM250k per depositor per member bank (pidm.gov.my, well-established statutory fact)

data.capital_basis: Principal-stable (fixed RM1.00 unit price); no negative distribution year on record; distribution non-guaranteed and declared annually
data.source: ASB declared distributions, this dataset rows asb-fy2021..asb-fy2025 (ASNB / The Edge / RinggitPlus, fetched 2026-06-15)

data.capital_basis: EPF Act 1991 §27 guarantees a minimum 2.5% nominal annual dividend on conventional savings; principal not exposed to market loss; illiquid until retirement/withdrawal conditions
data.job: Mandatory retirement floor with a statutory guaranteed minimum; long-lock
data.source: Owned corpus/data/datasets/epf-dividend-history.json (verified 2026-05-21): conventional declared 2022 5.35, 2023 5.50, 2024 6.30, 2025 6.15. EPF Act 1991 §27 guaranteed-minimum 2.5% dividend (statutory)

data.tier: Equity unit trust (risk-matched active fund band)
data.return_band_basis: Illustrative wide band, NOT guaranteed and can be NEGATIVE in any year. Anchored to broad Malaysian equity realized return (FBM KLCI capital return 1.2% pa 5y / 6.7% pa 3y, owned us-vs-my-returns.json) plus active-management dispersion (Public Mutual equity median weighted alpha +2.04 vs benchmark, Apr 2026, owned fund-universe-stats-snapshot.md). The band deliberately OVERLAPS the capital-stable tier — recent realized equity return did not exceed EPF/ASB.
data.downside_evidence: Universe median drawdown -3.93% from each fund's all-time-high NAV; deepest drawdown on file -49.32% (owned fund-universe-stats-snapshot.md, Apr 2026). Principal is at market risk; NAV fluctuates daily.
data.capital_basis: Full market risk; no capital guarantee; carries a sales charge and annual management fee (see corpus/data/datasets/sales-charge-by-channel.json, robo-fee-tiers.json)
data.job: Long-horizon growth potential and active alpha for money that can stay invested through drawdowns; a different job from the capital-stable tier, not a higher-return substitute for it
data.source: Owned corpus/data/datasets/us-vs-my-returns.json (FBM KLCI factsheet, FTSE Russell, as at 2026-05-29, fetched 2026-06-11) + owned corpus/proof/fund-universe-stats-snapshot.md (EngineerDad weighted-alpha screen, Apr 2026: equity median weighted alpha +2.04, median drawdown -3.93%, deepest -49.32%). No individual fund named or recommended (FIMM).
